name: Trail of the Dolmens of Los Gabrieles
name_original: Ruta de los dólmenes de Los Gabrieles — Valverde del Camino
description: Hike an easy 3.2-mile (5.1 km) route through a dehesa landscape to explore the ancient Dolmens of Los Gabrieles near Valverde del Camino.
region_intro: Hiking around Valverde Del Camino offers diverse landscapes, from pine forests and cork oak meadows to river valleys. The region, situated between the Tinto and Odiel rivers in the Huelva province, features varied terrain suitable for different hiking preferences. Hikers can explore ancient coniferous forests and routes alongside waterways, experiencing both natural beauty and historical elements. Elevation changes provide options ranging from easy paths to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Valverde Del Camino The most popular hiking route is Circular route along the Odiel River, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete, offering scenic views along the riverbanks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Odiel Mills River Trail, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path that winds through riverine landscapes and past historic water mills. Local hikers also love the Campanario Reservoir - Vía Verde de los Molinos del Agua Loop, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail leading through diverse environments including forests and a historic greenway, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Valverde Del Camino is defined by diverse landscapes, including pine forests, river valleys, and historic greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Valverde Del Camino are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Valverde Del Camino's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valverde Del Camino?
A: Valverde Del Camino offers a network of over 14 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes explore diverse landscapes, from river valleys to pine forests.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Valverde Del Camino?
A: Hikers in Valverde Del Camino will encounter a rich variety of landscapes, including serene countryside, dense pine forests, cork oak meadows, and eucalyptus groves. Many trails also follow picturesque river valleys, particularly along the Tinto and Odiel rivers and their tributaries.

Q: Are there hiking options for different skill levels?
A: Yes, the area provides trails for all abilities. There are 4 easy routes perfect for a relaxed stroll, 9 moderate trails offering a good challenge, and 1 difficult route for experienced hikers seeking more strenuous ascents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Valverde Del Camino?
A: Yes, many trails in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984001616/circular-route-along-the-odiel-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route along the Odiel River&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views and takes approximately 2 hours 54 minutes to complete.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore on a hike?
A: The trails often weave through areas rich in history. You can visit the prehistoric &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmens of Los Gabrieles&lt;/a&gt;, or explore routes that pass by the 18th-century Coronada bridge over the Odiel River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131649516/odiel-mills-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Mills River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past historic water mills.

Q: Are there trails that follow rivers or feature water elements?
A: Absolutely. The region is defined by the Tinto and Odiel rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131649516/odiel-mills-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Mills River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, winding through riverine landscapes. You might also encounter natural springs like Fuente del Cerrejón or Fuente de las Adelfillas along various routes.

Q: Are there any greenways or accessible paths suitable for easier walks?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5346190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Molino de Acha&lt;/a&gt; is a significant greenway, offering a 33.2 km path with an asphalt and compacted gravel surface. This route is ideal for easier walks, traversing diverse environments and passing by historic flour mills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144695715/trail-of-the-dolmens-of-los-gabrieles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Dolmens of Los Gabrieles&lt;/a&gt; is another easy option.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views in Valverde Del Camino?
A: For panoramic views, consider routes that include ascents like the Pico del Águila. While not a specific komoot route listed, the region's elevation changes provide opportunities for stunning vistas over the diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Valverde Del Camino?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements, and the well-documented trail network that caters to various preferences.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific parking locations for every trailhead are not detailed, areas like the ancient coniferous forests are equipped with infrastructure for visitors, suggesting parking facilities. The Casa Dirección, which houses an ethnographic museum and tourist information, also serves as a potential starting point for some routes, implying visitor amenities like parking.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144695715/trail-of-the-dolmens-of-los-gabrieles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Dolmens of Los Gabrieles&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.1 km path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a family outing to explore ancient history.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in natural areas are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Check local signage for any specific restrictions on individual routes.
tips: Nice and easy forest track that runs through the Gabrieles dolmens. These funerary vestiges are dated from the Bronze Age near the urban center of the town of Valverde del Camino.

faqs.question: Where does the Route of the dolmens of Los Gabrieles — Valverde del Camino trail start?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the municipality of Valverde del Camino in Huelva, Spain. While the exact trailhead isn't specified, it's generally accessed from the town, with an out-and-back option extending from there.

faqs.question: What can I see along this route?
faqs.answer: The primary attraction is the Megalithic Site of Los Gabrieles, a complex of six dolmens dating back to the Bronze Age. You'll also hike through a dehesa landscape with holm oaks and cork oaks, and may encounter grazing livestock like cows and pigs. Don't miss Dolmens of Los Gabrieles, the main highlight.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered easy and is suitable for all fitness levels and ages, including families. It's a pleasant walk with gentle, undulating profiles.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the dolmens route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows a forest track, which is generally easy to navigate. The landscape features a dehesa ecosystem with gentle, undulating profiles, characterized by holm oaks and cork oaks.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the dolmens of Los Gabrieles — Valverde del Camino dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through rural areas where livestock may be present. While not explicitly stated, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to ensure their safety and respect for local wildlife and farm animals.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to visit, as the fields are particularly vibrant and green, dotted with diverse flora. The pleasant weather also makes for a comfortable hiking experience.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to access the trail or the Megalithic Site of Los Gabrieles. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any specific dolmens worth paying extra attention to?
faqs.answer: Yes, while six dolmens originally existed, Dolmen 4, known as "La Encina," is considered the best preserved, retaining most of its vertical and horizontal pieces and part of its covering tumulus. Some dolmens even feature abstract or geometric engravings.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The route is described as a linear trail. An out-and-back option from Valverde del Camino can extend the total distance.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Zau and the Trialera Zau trails.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Valverde del Camino, a municipality in Huelva, Spain. You can expect to find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town itself.
